purpose: Services we KNOW exist on pay.sh but the agent cannot end-to-end use today. Three distinct sub-categories — check the `reason` and the matching `reasons.<code>` entry to know which: (1) CANNOT PAY (protocol/auth) — mpp_protocol / siwx_auth_required / invalid_demand. agent_pay fails at the protocol layer or refuses zero-demand. (2) CAN PAY BUT CANNOT DELIVER — requires_binary_response. agent_pay WOULD succeed and spend USDC, but the response (PNG/audio/video) can't be piped to Telegram/Discord as an attachment yet. (3) NOT 402 AT PROBE — endpoint_not_402_at_probe. Service is listed upstream but our probe got a non-402 status (4xx/5xx/200/301) — broken, moved, or auth-gated differently. Either way: NEVER call agent_pay on these. For (1) and (3) it fails for free. For (2) it would burn the user's USDC for nothing visible. Tell the user the service exists, explain the gap, and (if relevant) reference the BAT tracking the fix or suggest a re-probe.

mpp_protocol.label: Multi-Party Protocol (MPP)
mpp_protocol.explanation: Service uses pay.sh's newer Multi-Party Protocol settlement flow. Our X402Protocol implementation only supports the standard x402 v2 single-signer flow. Adding MPP support is non-trivial — separate BAT, not yet filed.

siwx_auth_required.label: Sign-In-With-X auth required
siwx_auth_required.explanation: Service requires a wallet auth handshake (sign-in-with-Solana style) before it will return a 402 with payment requirements. We don't implement that auth pre-flight yet. Tracked-adjacent in BAT-697 (Trigger V2 also needs SIWX) — once SIWX lands there, these become payable.

invalid_demand.label: Service returns 402 with amount=0 (no demand)
invalid_demand.explanation: Service returns HTTP 402 with amount=0 — semantically 'free' from x402's perspective, but still a 402 status. agent_pay refuses zero-demand by design, AND our web_fetch tool throws on any non-2xx response (so it can't fetch these either). Net result: not currently usable via our tools. Possible follow-up: a tool that fetches with 402-tolerant semantics, or a flag on web_fetch to allow 402 pass-through.

requires_binary_response.label: Service returns binary content (images / files) that we can't deliver
requires_binary_response.explanation: agent_pay parses x402 settles and returns the response body as best-effort UTF-8 text. Services that return binary content (e.g. PNG screenshots, audio, video) can be paid but the binary bytes can't currently be piped to the Telegram/Discord channel as an attachment — there's no in-memory-buffer-to-channel path. Tracked: needs a follow-up BAT to (a) save binary content to a workspace file and (b) plumb that file path to the channel sender. Until then, tell the user the service is recognized but its output format isn't supported yet.

endpoint_not_402_at_probe.label: Service exists in pay.sh's catalog but didn't return a 402 at probe time
endpoint_not_402_at_probe.explanation: Service is listed in pay.sh's upstream catalog but our probe got a non-402 HTTP status (4xx/5xx, sometimes 200/301). Possible causes: endpoint moved, broken, auth-gated differently, or re-routed to a free response. agent_pay needs a 402 to settle, so it can't pay these. web_fetch could in theory reach a 2xx response, but the agent doesn't know the request shape and shouldn't probe blindly. Each entry's `note` field records the probe-time HTTP status as a hint. Tell the user the service is listed upstream but not currently reachable through our tooling.

services.name: paysponge/screenshotone
services.note: Animated/static screenshots return PNG/GIF bytes. Paid+detect+build works; delivery to user does not.

services.name: paysponge/coingecko
services.note: http_404 at 2026-05-14 probe — endpoint removed; replaced upstream by stablecrypto/market-data which is in catalog.json.

services.name: paysponge/perplexity
services.note: http_200 at 2026-05-14 probe — returned 2xx without 402; unknown paid surface.

services.name: paysponge/textbelt
services.note: Textbelt's status endpoint is free; the POST send endpoint IS paid and IS in our supported list under… (none — POST endpoint exists but isn't part of pay.sh's catalog entry, so it never appears here)
